rulings.Case Title: WEST BUILDERS, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. MUSEUM PLAZA, LLC., A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY et al

rulings.Rulings: MATTER ON CALENDAR ON MONDAY, APRIL 21, 2014, LINE 2, DEFENDANT MUSEUM PLAZA, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY Motion To Lift Stay. The motion to lift stay is denied. Moving party has not provided a statutory basis to lift the existing stay in this action. This motion is denied without prejudice to the moving party filing a petition to compel arbitration (in a new action) pursuant to CCP 1281.2, and, if it wishes, seeking to consolidate separate arbitration proceedings pursuant to CCP 1281.3 or such other relief. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: MR WIRE COMMUNICATIONS DBA MR WIRE VS. REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: The Regents Of The University Of California'S Motion For Leave To File Cross Complaint
rulings.Rulings: MATTER ON CALENDAR ON MONDAY, APRIL 21, 2014, LINE 16, The Regents Of The University Of California'S Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint. Granted, pursuant to CCP section 426.50. Defendant The Regents of the University of California ("Regents") failure to file a cross complaint was in good faith. No trial date is yet set. A motion for leave to file a compulsory cross-complaint "shall be liberally construed to avoid forfeiture of causes of action." CCP sec. 426.50. Plaintiff Mr. Wire has not established that Regents cannot state a valid cause of action in the proposed cross-complaint. Regents may file its proposed cross complaint within 10 days of entry of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: MR WIRE COMMUNICATIONS DBA MR WIRE VS. REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Bay Area Systems & Solutions, Inc.'S Demurrer To 1st Amended Cross Complaint
rulings.Rulings: MATTER ON CALENDAR ON MONDAY, APRIL 21, 2014, LINE 16, Bay Area Systems & Solutions, Inc.'s Demurrer To 1st Amended Cross-Complaint. Overruled in part and sustained in part. Cross-Defendant Bay Area Systems & Solutions, Inc.'s ("Bass") request for judicial notice is granted. As to the first cause of action for express indemnity, the demurrer is overruled. The First Amended Cross-Complaint (FACC) pleads that the owner of the property has assessed liquidated damages against Cross-Complainant Ranger Pipelines, Inc. ("Ranger"), (FACC, par. 16.) The FACC also pleads that the subcontract provides that Cross-Defendant Bass must indemnify Cross-Complainant Ranger "from and against all actions, penalties, assessments¿demands, liabilities, claims, damages, costs, losses, and expenses." (FACC, par. 9, Ex. A.) A general demurrer does not lie to only part of a cause of action and therefore the demurrer is overruled to this cause of action in its entirety. (See Kong v. City of Hawaiin Gardens Redevelop. Agency (2003) 108 Cal.App.4th 1028, 1047.) As to the second cause of action for declaratory relief, the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. (CCP sec. 430.10(e).) The "actual controversies" alleged in the FACC have already "ripened into a violation of a contractual obligation." (Rimington v. General Ace. Group of Ins. Cos. (1962) 205 Cal.App.2d 394, 397.) The underlying Second Amended Complaint filed by Plaintiff Mr. Wire Communications is based on allegations that Cross-Complainant Ranger provided defective designs and the resultant damages have already occurred. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: SARAH DUPONT VS. LAUREEN SCHILLER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Attendance At Depo
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, April 21, 2014, Line 1: DEFENDANTS LAUREEN SCHILLER, ROBERT SCHILLER, AND NICHOLAS SCHILLER'S Motion To Compel Attendance At Deposition Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted no opposition. Plaintiff to appear for deposition pursuant to the terms of the prior notice of deposition and demand to produce at the law offices of defense counsel at a mutually convenient time within 10 business days of service of this order upon her. Plaintiff to pay sanctions of $600 at the conclusion of the case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rkane1089@aol.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: LFG NATIONAL CAPITAL, LLC VS. JOSEPH M. ALIOTO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Quash Subpoenas Served Upon Fremont Bank. Mada Enterprises, Llc Marin Mortgage Bankers, Michele Alioto And Lupori & Associates, Pursuant To Ccp Sec 1987.1
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, April 21, 2014, Line 3: DEFENDANTS JOSEPH ALIOTO'S and THE ALIOTO LAW FIRM'S, a sole proprietorship, Motion To Quash Subpoenas Served Upon Fremont Bank, Mada Enterprises, LLC, Marin Mortgage Bankers, Michele Alioto, snd Lupori & Associates, Pursuant To CCP Sec 1987.1 Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear to address the applicability of Code of Civ. Proc. §485.230 which appears to only apply if a right to attach order was issued. Since this appears inapplicable, how are all the financial records requested relevant to the subject matter of the litigation. The pendency of summary judgment motion should not prevent plaintiff from undertaking discovery that is relevant to subject matter of litigation so long as it is not overbroad or otherwise seeks privileged documents. Assuming some discovery is ordered, what, if any, limitations should be imposed. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ACCESS PUBLIC RELATIONS LLC VS. DEEM INC. (FKA REARDEN COMMERCE INC.)
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: MATTER ON CALENDAR ON MONDAY, APRIL 21, 2014, LINE 8, CROSS-DEFENDANT ACCESS PUBLIC RELATIONS LLC DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT. Overruled. The First Amended Cross-Complaint ("FACC") states that "Access made representations that it possessed the experience, qualifications, and personnel necessary to produce the services and work product according to Deem's specifications." (FACC, 12.) As pled, this statement is not merely advertising puffery. Rather, Cross-Defendant affirmatively represented that it had the ability to perform a specific job. Pulvers v. Kaiser Foundation Health Plan (1979) 99 Cal. App. 3d 560, 564, relied on by moving party, is inapposite. In that case, the doctor's statement that he would provide a "good result" was in the context of a breach of warranty action, not fraud. rulings.Case Title: HIN QIN XU BY AND THROUGH HER GUARDIAN AD LITEM VS. TRANSIT AMERICA SERVICES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: MATTER ON CALENDAR ON MONDAY, APRIL 21, 2014, LINE 9, DEFENDANT STATE OF CALIFORNIA DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained, with 10 days leave to amend. (CCP § 430.10(e).) As to the first cause of action for negligence and the second cause of action for dangerous condition of public property, the demurrer is sustained with leave to amend to state a statutory basis for relief against Defendant State of California ("Defendant"). (See Lopez v. Southern Cal. Rapid Transit Dist. (1985) 40 Cal.3d 780, 795 ("statutory causes of action must be pleaded with particularity is applicable").) The third and fourth grounds for demurrer (as stated in Defendant's Notice of and Demurrer to First Amended Complaint) are overruled. For these grounds, Defendant relies on a request for judicial notice of extrinsic evidence to show that Defendant Kate Cuevas is not a state employee and that Defendant does not own or control the property at issue. The request for judicial notice of the truth of the matters asserted in documents on websites is denied. (Searles Valley Minerals Operations, Inc. v. State Bd. of Equalization (2008) 160 Cal.App.4th 514, 519 (affirming denial of request for judicial notice of materials contained on the website pages of the American Coal Foundation and the U.S. Department of Energy).) The third and fourth grounds for demurrer thus raise questions of fact that are not the proper basis for a demurrer. The fifth ground for demurrer, pertaining to the Second Cause of Action for Dangerous Condition, is overruled. Plaintiff alleges a dangerous condition on both the railroad tracks and the adjacent property. (Gov. Code section 830(a); FAC paras. 19-21.). The sixth ground for demurrer (failure to identify any physical defect) is overruled because Defendant does not make an argument in support of that ground in its moving papers. To the extent that it is part of the fifth ground for demurrer, it is overruled for the reasons stated above. The seventh ground for demurrer is overruled. The allegations of the FAC do not appear to implicate Government Code section 818.2 and 845; in any event, a demurrer will not lie to only part of a cause of action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: FRANKLIN H WRIGHT VS. MARRIOTT INTERNATIONAL, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: MATTER ON CALENDAR ON MONDAY, APRIL 21, 2014, LINE 11, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant City and County of San Francisco's Demurrer to Causes of Action 5-11 in Plaintiff's Complaint is sustained with leave to amend. The face of the Complaint demonstrates that Plaintiff has not complied with the claim filing requirements of the Government Claims Act prior to filing the Complaint. Plaintiff is given leave to amend, if he can do so in good faith, to plead that he has met the requirements of the Government Claims Act, including Government Code sections 910 and section 915(e). See DiCampli-Mintz v. County of Santa Clara (2012) 55 Cal 4th 983, 991-992. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: URIEL SCOTT VS. TRADEHILL INC, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: MATTER ON CALENDAR ON MONDAY, APRIL 21, 2014, LINE 13, DEFENDANT TRADEHILL, INC. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The demurrer is overruled in its entirety. The Complaint states facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action and there is no misjoinder of parties. (CCP 430.10(e) and 430.10(e). Pre-incorporation contracts are enforceable. The Complaint makes clear that the contract is written. See Paragraph 39. (CCP 430.10(g). Finally, as to the demurrer to the fourth cause of action, Plaintiff sufficiently alleges the terms of the contract according to their legal effect. If Plaintiff seeks to name Doe defendants, Plaintiff shall make a formal ex parte application and present it to Department 302, pursuant to CCP 474. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: AUDIO VISUAL MANAGEMENT GROUP, INC. VS. NICHOLAS MEEKS
rulings.Rulings: MATTER ON CALENDAR ON MONDAY, APRIL 21, 2014, LINE 15, APPELLANT AUDIO VISUAL MANAGEMENT GROUP, INC., ABSOLUTE PRODUCTION SERVICES, MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-14-537503, CGC-14-537506, CGC-14-537507, CGC-14-537513, CGC-14-537517, CGC-14-537520, CGC-14-537525, CGC-14-537528, CGC-14-537534, CGC-14-537535 WITH CASE CGC-14-537500. Continued to April 22, 2014 to give moving party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Moving party must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than April 21, 2014 at 9:30 am or the motion will be denied. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the Joint Notice of Motion and Motion, the opposition and the reply papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. = (302/MJM)
